steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The passage presents two opposing claims about Saville: “is not the protagonist” versus “she's the 'occasion'”—contrasting roles that require proper punctuation to express their relationship clearly.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
When two independent clauses are separated by a conjunctive adverb, the pattern is: semicolon + adverb + comma.

The choice “novel; rather,” correctly applies this pattern to separate “Saville is not the protagonist of Shelley's novel” and “she's the 'occasion' of it.” The other choices either omit the semicolon or the word “rather” entirely. This correct punctuation establishes the precise relationship between the two opposing roles. That matches choice C — C is correct.
